rationale: Replace naive breakout with funding-rate-aware momentum. Entry requires both price above SMA (momentum) and funding z-score below threshold (not crowded longs). Exit triggers when funding becomes extreme positive (crowded positioning, correction risk) or price drops below trailing low. This exploits the structural pressure mechanism: extreme positive funding bleeds long holders, creating reversion.

Switch from funding z-score to percentile rank (bounded 0-1, more regime-stable). Slow the momentum filter from SMA(48) to SMA(168) (7-day) to reduce false entries in choppy markets. Entry requires funding percentile below median (not crowded). Exit on funding becoming extreme (>85th percentile) or loss of momentum (close < SMA). The goal is to drastically reduce variance: std_sharpe=2.65 is the main fitness drag.

Switch from funding z-score to percentile rank (bounded 0-1, more regime-stable). Slow the momentum filter from SMA(48) to SMA(168) (7-day) to reduce false entries in choppy markets. Entry requires funding percentile below median (not crowded). Exit on funding becoming extreme (>85th percentile) or price dropping below trailing 2-day low. The goal is to drastically reduce std_sharpe=2.65 which is the main fitness drag.

W5 is the only negative window (-1.089). The trailing low exit causes whipsaw in choppy recent markets. Switch to SMA-based exit (close < SMA) for consistency with entry signal — when momentum is lost, exit. Slightly relax entry threshold to 0.55 and use SMA(120) for faster adaptation. This should improve W5 without breaking the other four positive windows.

Parametric tuning to improve W5 (-1.089). Widen trailing stop from 48 to 72 bars (3 days) to reduce whipsaw exits in choppy recent markets. Tighten entry threshold from 0.50 to 0.45 to be more selective (only enter when funding is clearly below median). Keep SMA(168) and fr_exit_pct(0.85) which are working well for W1-W4.

Parametric tuning to improve W5 (-1.089). Widen trailing stop from 48 to 72 bars (3 days) to reduce whipsaw exits in choppy recent markets. Tighten entry threshold from 0.50 to 0.45 to be more selective. Keep SMA(168) and fr_exit_pct(0.85) which are working well for W1-W4.

Add funding momentum filter to address W5 weakness. Enter only when funding percentile is below median AND funding rate is declining (negative 3-period diff). Declining funding means crowded positions are unwinding — structural pressure is actively creating opportunity. This should filter out entries during periods of rising funding (building crowd) that tend to precede corrections. Keep SMA(168) momentum and trailing low exit from the accepted v2 strategy.

Replace fixed trailing low exit with ATR-based adaptive stop. The fixed 48-bar trailing low gets triggered on noise in W5's choppy market. ATR-scaled exit adjusts automatically: tighter in low-vol, wider in high-vol. Entry unchanged (SMA momentum + funding percentile filter). ATR stop: exit when close drops below recent_low minus atr_mult * ATR. This should preserve W1-W4 performance while adapting to W5's volatility regime.

Try pure funding mean-reversion: enter ONLY when funding is at extreme lows (below 20th percentile — shorts crowded, squeeze risk) combined with price not collapsing (above long SMA). Exit when funding normalizes above 60th percentile (opportunity passed) or trailing stop hit. The mechanism is strongest at extremes, and extreme negative funding is the clearest reducible signal. This targets fewer, higher-quality entries rather than many marginal ones.

Gentle parametric tuning. Shorten funding percentile window from 720 to 480 (20-day instead of 30-day) for more responsive regime adaptation. Lower exit threshold from 0.85 to 0.80 to exit crowded positions slightly earlier. These small changes should improve consistency across windows without breaking the strategy structure.

Single-variable change: raise fr_entry_pct from 0.50 to 0.55 to allow entries when funding is slightly above median. This may help W5 by permitting entries that were previously filtered out due to funding being slightly elevated. All other parameters unchanged.

Continue exploring the fr_entry_pct axis which yielded the +0.49 Δ. Nudge from 0.55 to 0.58 to allow slightly more entries. The 0.50→0.55 change fixed W5 dramatically; 0.58 may further improve W1 (+0.214, weakest positive window) by allowing additional quality entries. All other params unchanged.

Explore exit axis: raise fr_exit_pct from 0.85 to 0.90 to hold positions longer before the funding-crowding exit triggers. W1 (+0.214) may benefit from holding winning positions through moderate funding increases rather than exiting too early. Single-variable change from the accepted 0.55 strategy.

W1 (+0.214) is the weakest window. Try SMA(144) instead of SMA(168) — 6-day vs 7-day momentum. A slightly faster SMA may detect momentum shifts earlier in W1's choppy period (Mar-Jun 2024), potentially generating better-timed entries. Single-variable change.

Try tighter trailing stop: exit_lookback from 48 to 36 (1.5 days). In W1 (29% WR, 7 trades), cutting losers faster could preserve more capital. The wider exit lets losing positions bleed too long in choppy conditions. Single-variable change from the +0.6110 accepted baseline.

Continue tightening trailing stop: exit_lookback 36→30. The 48→36 move was accepted (+0.19). This may improve W1 (+0.057) and W2 by cutting losers even faster, but risks hurting W3/W4 which benefit from holding through dips. Testing the boundary.
exit_lookback 30 was too tight, 36 was accepted. Try 42 as a middle ground that might balance W1 preservation with W3-W5 gains. The optimal trailing stop length is somewhere in the 36-48 range.

Try SMA(192) — 8-day momentum. The current 7-day SMA may be too fast for W1's choppy period. A slower SMA produces fewer entry signals in choppy conditions, potentially improving W1 (+0.057) by filtering out marginal entries. W2-W4 have strong enough trends that a slightly slower SMA should still capture them.

SMA(192) was a big win (+0.20). Try SMA(216) — 9-day momentum — to see if the trend continues. W5 (+0.107) could benefit from even fewer entries in the recent choppy market. The risk is W2-W3 losing entries during their strong trends.

Fine-tune fr_entry_pct: try 0.52 (between the original 0.50 and the current 0.55). The 0.50→0.55 jump was transformative, but 0.55 may admit some marginal entries that hurt W5. 0.52 could be the sweet spot that keeps the W5 improvement while filtering out the weakest entries.

Fine-tune fr_exit_pct from 0.90 to 0.92. The current 0.90 exit works well but may exit some W4/W5 positions slightly early. A higher threshold lets winning positions run longer during moderate funding increases before triggering the crowding exit.

Try exit_lookback from 42 to 38. The 36→42 range was explored, and 36 was accepted with good results. 38 might be a better sweet spot — tighter stops help cut losses in W5 (Sharpe +0.314, lowest window) while 42 was slightly too loose for the choppy recent market.

Final iteration: try fr_entry_pct from 0.52 to 0.53. The 0.50→0.55 range has been explored and 0.52 was the last accepted value. A small nudge to 0.53 might marginally improve W5 (+0.571) without hurting other windows. Conservative single-variable change.
